raw_text:
Lead Product Manager

Velocity Labs — Empowering teams to ship products faster with intelligent analytics and collaboration

tools

Location

San Francisco, CA

Employment Type

Full-time

Compensation

$185,000 - $240,000 USD

The Opportunity

We're seeking an experienced Lead Product Manager to own our core Analytics Platform and lead a

growing team of 4-5 product engineers. In this role, you'll set product strategy, drive quarterly roadmap

decisions, mentor junior product managers, and work cross-functionally with design and engineering

leadership to deliver products that delight users. You'll have direct P&L responsibility and shape the
future direction of Velocity Labs' flagship offering.

About Velocity Labs

Velocity Labs is a Series B SaaS company founded in 2019 that provides a unified platform for product
teams to make data-driven decisions. We combine real-time analytics, feature flagging, and team
collaboration tools to help companies reduce time-to-market and improve product quality. With a strong

focus on developer experience and product excellence, Velocity Labs serves over 500 companies
ranging from early-stage startups to Fortune 500 enterprises.

Day-to-Day

Set product strategy and vision for the Analytics Platform, defining 12-month roadmap in alignment

with company OKRs and working directly with Chief Product Officer on quarterly planning

Lead, mentor, and develop a team of 4-5 product engineers; conduct 1-on-1s, provide career
development guidance, and support performance reviews

Design and run discovery processes using SQL queries, Amplitude and Mixpanel analytics to identify
user pain points and validate feature opportunities with quantitative data

Partner with Engineering leadership to define technical roadmap trade-offs, decompose features into
milestones, and ensure sustainable velocity and code quality

Work closely with Design to iterate on user workflows, review prototypes, and ensure data-informed
UX decisions through A/B testing and user research synthesis

Drive adoption and retention metrics through feature launches, internal analytics reviews (weekly),

and cross-functional syncs with Sales and Customer Success teams

Present quarterly business reviews to company leadership and key customers, articulating product

strategy, competitive positioning, and customer success stories

 Own the relationships with top enterprise customers and design solutions that unblock their most

critical use cases

Establish product best practices and frameworks across the team, including roadmap prioritization,

requirements documentation, and post-launch analysis

You Have

6+ years of product management experience, with at least 2+ years in a lead or senior product

manager role managing product teams or large strategic initiatives

Proven expertise with product analytics tools (Amplitude, Mixpanel, or similar) and SQL for writing

queries to extract insights from product data

Strong technical acumen—ability to read code, understand system architecture, and make informed

trade-offs between UX simplicity and technical feasibility

Track record of shipping products that drive adoption, revenue, or key business metrics; ideally SaaS

B2B experience

Excellent communication and presentation skills, comfortable presenting to executives, engineering
teams, and customers in both formal and informal settings

Experience with A/B testing frameworks and statistical rigor in decision-making; familiarity with tools

like Statsig, LaunchDarkly, or equivalent feature flagging platforms

People management experience—ability to hire, mentor, and develop talent; demonstrated success
growing team members into stronger product thinkers

Bonus

Experience building data infrastructure or analytics products; familiarity with SQL data warehouses
(Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift) and ETL concepts

Background working cross-functionally with Design teams on user research and usability testing

methodologies

Startup or scale-up experience with exposure to multiple business models or GTM motions

Familiarity with Figma, product roadmapping tools (Jira, Asana, Productboard), and Slack API
integrations

Our Stack

Amplitude   Mixpanel   SQL   Figma   Jira   Statsig   LaunchDarkly   Asana   Looker   Slack

What We Offer

Competitive salary + equity package with industry-standard 4-year vesting and 1-year cliff

Comprehensive health insurance (medical, dental, vision) with 90% of premiums covered for
employees and 75% for dependents

Unlimited PTO with a minimum of 20 days encouraged; separate parental leave policy (16 weeks
paid)

 Professional development budget: $2,500/year for conferences, courses, and coaching; we encourage

monthly team lunches with guest speakers

Home office stipend ($1,000 annually) and company laptop (MacBook Pro or Dell XPS, your choice)

Mental health support: Calm app subscription + quarterly virtual counseling sessions covered by

insurance

Monthly team offsites (hybrid company) and annual in-person company retreat in Napa Valley

Commuter benefits and pre-tax FSA/HSA options to maximize take-home pay

Apply

Apply via our careers page at velocitylabs.com/careers or send your resume and a one-page cover letter

explaining why you're excited about the role to careers@velocitylabs.com. Please also include a link to

your portfolio or 1-2 sample product presentations (slides or video). We review applications on a rolling

basis and aim to respond within 1 week. Top candidates will participate in a structured interview process:

(1) Hiring manager deep-dive on product thinking and past experience, (2) Product strategy case study,

(3) Cross-functional panel with engineering lead and design director, (4) Final conversation with CPO.
nice_to_have: Experience building data infrastructure or analytics products; familiarity with SQL data warehouses
Background working cross-functionally with Design teams on user research and usability testing
Startup or scale-up experience with exposure to multiple business models or GTM motions
Familiarity with Figma, product roadmapping tools (Jira, Asana, Productboard), and Slack API
